# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.114.0/http/server.ts";

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Module path not provided", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const moduleUrl = new URL(modulePath);
    if (moduleUrl.protocol !== "https:") {
      throw new Error("Only HTTPS modules are allowed");
    }

    const module = await import(moduleUrl.href);
    if (typeof module.default !== "function") {
      throw new Error("Module does not export a default function");
    }

    const result = await module.default();
    return new Response(result, { status: 200 });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(`Error loading module: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(handleRequest);
